completion_criteria_checked: PRE-RUN RECORD: proposed Class A, self-close not attempted this run. -- MET: recorded in DECISION-056 before either cycle 1 or cycle 2 started.

New read-only script reports counts of all ten known event types. -- MET: re-read scripts/summarize-routing-log.ps1 in full; independently re-ran it myself (as verifier, in a freshly created scratch copy, not reusing the worker's) against the real, current routing-log.jsonl and got identical output to what worker-result.md claims.
Computes exactly one ratio (claimed-vs-verified completion rate), nothing else. -- MET: only one ratio is computed in the code; verified the arithmetic by hand (26/27 = 96.3%).
Explicitly reports the three un-instrumented metrics rather than approximating them. -- MET: read directly in the script's final output block; no proxy computation exists in the code for these three.
No state mutation; read-only. -- MET: re-read the full script; the only file operations are Test-Path and Get-Content, both read-only. No Set-Content, no Add-Content, no call to any other script.
No new event type, no log-event.ps1 call. -- MET: confirmed by reading the script and by grep -- 'log-event' does not appear anywhere in the new file.

Functionally tested against real (copied) log content, arithmetically cross-checked. -- MET AND INDEPENDENTLY REPEATED: re-ran the script myself against a separately-created scratch copy of the real log; output matched the worker's claimed output exactly. Cross-checked several of the counts by hand against grep -c on the real log file directly.
A new decision recorded. -- MET: DECISION-058 present, accurately describes the real defect-and-fix.

out_of_scope_findings: None found. git status --short confirms the only new file is scripts/summarize-routing-log.ps1; the only other changes are docs/DECISIONS.md plus the expected live state/handoff/result files. No existing script or schema touched.
risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback. No independent second-party (Codex) review has occurred. GPT review not yet performed on this specific cycle.
PILOT DESIGN (DECISION-056): proposed Class A, but self-close not attempted this run regardless. This is a HELD candidate, alongside pcc-brr4-002 -- neither has been closed out.

Verification Depth Policy: even though this task is Class A (which would normally warrant lighter 'light' or 'normal' depth per the Verification Depth Policy's own table), I applied a fuller check here anyway -- independently re-running the script myself rather than only reading the code -- because this is a held pilot cycle under active second-party review, not an ordinary Class A self-accept.

The legacy-format handling (26 of 70 log lines) is a real, disclosed finding about the repo's own history, not a flaw introduced by this task -- but it does mean routing-log.jsonl itself is not a fully uniform data source, a fact this script surfaces honestly rather than papering over.

This script's 'known event types' list is a hardcoded snapshot of log-event.ps1's current ValidateSet; if a future task adds another event type to log-event.ps1 without also updating this script, that new type would silently fall into the 'unrecognized' bucket rather than get its own line. Disclosed here, not fixed (fixing it would mean deriving the list from log-event.ps1's ValidateSet directly, a reasonable future improvement not required by this task's narrow scope).
summary: Verified pcc-brr4-003 (cycle 2, final cycle of pilot run #2) with an independent re-run of the script itself, not just a code read-through -- created my own fresh scratch copy and reproduced the worker's exact output against the real log, then spot-checked several counts by hand with grep. The script is genuinely read-only, strictly mechanical (raw counts plus one named ratio, explicit non-computation of three un-instrumented metrics), and the real legacy-format defect found during the worker's own testing was fixed honestly rather than papered over with an invented mapping. All ten completion criteria are met with direct, independently-reproduced evidence. This held PASS candidate, together with pcc-brr4-002's, is submitted for actual owner/GPT review -- pilot run #2 (both cycles) is complete and awaiting that review, not self-declared successful.
next_action: This is a HELD result, not a close-out (per DECISION-056). Do not run scripts/close-out-verified-task.ps1 for either pcc-brr4-002 or pcc-brr4-003 until reviewed.
